title: Asian stocks are mixed after big tech sell-off
description: Asian stocks are mixed on Wednesday, following a sell-off in big technology stocks from Asia to Wall Street. U.S. stock futures are also trading mixed. Global investors are monitoring market movements in Japan and South Korea, which have seen big gains in recent months on the global AI boom but both fell sharply on Tuesday. South Korea's benchmark Kospi index was up 0.5% on Wednesday, while Japan's Nikkei 225 lost 1.1%. On Wall Street, the benchmark S&P 500 fell 1.4% Tuesday. The tech-heavy Nasdaq composite dropped 2.2%, while the Dow was 0.1% lower. Oil prices fell as more ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz and as U.S.-Iran talks on a permanent end to the Iran war progressed.

title: MapLight Therapeutics Announces Changes to Board of Directors
description: SAN FRANCISCO and BOSTON, June 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- MapLight Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: MPLT), a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on improving the lives of patients suffering from debilitating central nervous system (CNS) disorders, today announced the appointment of Martin Babler and Troy Cox to its Board of Directors following their election at the Company’s Annual Meeting of Stockholders on June 23, 2026. Mr. Babler and Mr. Cox succeed Robert Malenka, M.D., Ph.D., and Jim Trenkle, Ph.D., who did not stand for re-election and have concluded their service on the Board. Mr. Babler has been appointed as a member of the Audit Committee, and Mr. Cox has been appointed Chair of the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee.
